habitat: Open grassy fields; farmlands; cities; moorland; heathland; grassland; and savanna
story: Meet the Common Kestral (Falco tinnunculus), a distinctive bird from the Falconidae family. Preferring habitats such as Open grassy fields; farmlands; cities; moorland; heathland; grassland; and savanna, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Common Kestral serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Common Kestral enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
